title: [ESI][Runtime] Add Debug DLLs to wheels for Windows
body:
Windows applications in Debug mode crash when linking against the ESI Runtime wheel due to runtime library mismatches. Only Release DLLs were being packaged.

## Solution

Build and package both Release and Debug configurations of all C++ libraries and tools on Windows in a single wheel.

## Changes

### Build process (`setup.py`)
- **Dual configuration build on Windows**: Builds Release first, then Debug in separate directories
- **Release build**: Full build with `cmake --install` for ESIRuntime component
- **Debug build**: Only builds C++ runtime and tools (skips Python extension due to nanobind CRT incompatibility)
- **Manual installation for Debug**: Copies specific debug DLLs, import libraries (.lib), PDBs, and executables to wheel
- **Non-Windows platforms**: Unchanged (single Release build)

### Library naming (`CMakeLists.txt`, `python/CMakeLists.txt`)
- Set `DEBUG_POSTFIX "_d"` for all Windows targets:
  - C++ libraries: `ESICppRuntime_d.dll`, `CosimRpc_d.dll`, `CosimBackend_d.dll`
  - Tools: `esiquery_d.exe`
- Install PDB files for all targets (marked `OPTIONAL`)
- **Python extension**: Disabled in MSVC Debug builds due to nanobind CRT incompatibility
  - Only Release version (`esiCppAccel.pyd`) included in wheel
  - Works with both Debug and Release applications

### Backend plugin loading (`cpp/lib/Accelerator.cpp`)
- Added runtime detection for Debug builds on Windows
- Uses `_DEBUG` preprocessor macro to load `*Backend_d.dll` in Debug mode
- Ensures backend plugins match application build configuration

### CMake config (`esiaccelConfig.cmake.in`)
- Simplified configuration detection (removed problematic runtime detection)
- **Default behavior**: Uses Release DLLs (single-config generators)
- **Multi-config generators** (Visual Studio, Xcode): Uses configuration-specific properties
  - `IMPORTED_LOCATION_RELEASE` → `ESICppRuntime.dll`
  - `IMPORTED_LOCATION_DEBUG` → `ESICppRuntime_d.dll`
- Applied to all three libraries: ESICppRuntime, CosimRpc, CosimBackend

### Other changes
- Removed `esitester` from ESIRuntime dependencies (not installed or included in wheels)

## Usage

### C++ Applications
```cmake
find_package(esiaccel REQUIRED)
target_link_libraries(myapp esiaccel::ESICppRuntime)
```
- Debug build → `ESICppRuntime_d.dll`, `CosimBackend_d.dll`
- Release build → `ESICppRuntime.dll`, `CosimBackend.dll`
- CMake automatically selects based on build configuration

### Python Applications
- **All Python applications** use `esiCppAccel.pyd` (Release version only)
- The Release Python extension works correctly with both Debug and Release C++ applications
- Debug Python interpreters are not officially supported by this wheel

## Files Changed
- `lib/Dialect/ESI/runtime/CMakeLists.txt` - Debug postfix and PDB installation
- `lib/Dialect/ESI/runtime/python/CMakeLists.txt` - Disable in MSVC Debug
- `lib/Dialect/ESI/runtime/setup.py` - Dual build logic
- `lib/Dialect/ESI/runtime/cpp/lib/Accelerator.cpp` - Debug plugin loading
- `lib/Dialect/ESI/runtime/cpp/cmake/esiaccelConfig.cmake.in` - Configuration properties
